page: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-48 is a general-purpose server powered by an Intel x86_64 processor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs derived from 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, alongside 192 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in memory bandwidth and specialized CPU instructions. The server achieves top-tier read-only memory bandwidth of 3417.19 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 259.28 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, alongside a write bandwidth of 114.19 GB/sec. It demonstrates strong performance in Passmark CPU tests, particularly in extended instructions, encryption, and physics. Additionally, the processor supports Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512), which contribute to strong prompt processing speeds during LLM inference, reaching 2557.3 tokens/sec for small models and 23.69 tokens/sec for large 70B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n4-standard-48 offers a balanced resource density suitable for enterprise workloads without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. However, the lack of local NVMe storage means workloads requiring high-speed local disk I/O must rely on network-attached storage, which introduces a performance tradeoff. This server is well-suited for database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-48 is a general-purpose server featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192 GB of RAM on an Intel x86_64 architecture. Powered by 24 physical cores with hyper-threading, it delivers a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core and a substantial 260 MB L3 cache. The server lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, but excels in memory bandwidth, showing strong performance in Passmark CPU tests, database operations, and static web serving. It features advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512, which drive strong prompt processing speeds for large language models. Qualitatively, it offers high resource density for general-purpose workloads, though the absence of local storage requires network-attached alternatives. It is highly suited for database hosting, web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-48 is a general-purpose server with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and an Intel x86_64 architecture, offering strong memory bandwidth and database performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-48 features 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and Intel x86_64 architecture for balanced general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring dedicated Intel processors, high memory bandwidth, and advanced matrix extensions for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs and 192 GB of RAM on Intel x86_64 architecture.
Strong memory bandwidth and Passmark database performance.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated CPU-based LLM inference.
No local storage or bundled GPUs, requiring network-attached storage.
Optimized for database operations, web serving, and software compilation.
